before.title: MPEG-DASH MPD Python Library
before.description: A Python implementation of the MPEG-DASH schema and tools to validate or create MPD files. It aligns with the official XML schemas for DASH, helping generate standard-compliant manifests for testing or production.

after.title: MPEG-DASH MPD XML Schema (DASHSchema)
after.description: Official XML Schema (XSD) definitions and example MPD files for the MPEG-DASH Media Presentation Description, maintained by MPEG's DASH working group for validating standards-compliant manifests.

BUG-041.before:
A rewrite of the interface and orchestration layers of FFmpeg. Includes...
* Graphical user interface that makes it easy… (946 chars)
BUG-041.after: A modern re-imagining of FFmpeg's interface and orchestration layers for the AI era, pairing the proven FFmpeg engine with a cleaner command surface, job orchestration, and automation-friendly workflows for transcoding at scale.

BUG-040.title: How Do I Become an ANSI Member
BUG-040.after: Information about joining ANSI, the American National Standards Institute, which develops and maintains technical standards across various industries.

BUG-040.title: Avtk
BUG-040.after: A Python library for audio and video processing that provides tools for encoding and manipulating media files.

BUG-040.title: Ffmpeg Python (kkroening)
BUG-040.after: A Python wrapper for FFmpeg that simplifies interaction with the FFmpeg command-line tool for encoding, decoding, and processing video and audio.

BUG-040.title: Ffmpy
BUG-040.after: A lightweight Python wrapper for FFmpeg that enables simple encoding and transcoding of video and audio files.

BUG-040.title: Moviepy
BUG-040.after: A Python library for video editing and composition that uses FFmpeg backend to create, edit, and manipulate video files programmatically.

BUG-040.title: Objective Perceptual Analysis
BUG-040.after: A perceptual video quality assessment tool that analyzes and measures objective quality metrics for video content evaluation.

BUG-040.title: Libgosubs
BUG-040.after: A library for parsing and manipulating subtitle files in various formats for processing and conversion.

BUG-040.title: Mp
BUG-040.after: A media inspection and analysis tool that provides information and metadata about media files.

BUG-040.title: Qctools
BUG-040.after: A quality control tool for analyzing video files, detecting defects, and evaluating technical quality metrics for archival and broadcast purposes.

BUG-040.title: Flowblade
BUG-040.after: A free, open-source video editor for cutting, compositing, and editing video with a timeline-based interface.

BUG-040.title: Hlstools
BUG-040.after: Tools for working with HLS streams, including parsing, inspection, and manipulation of HTTP Live Streaming manifest and segment files.

BUG-040.title: Lossless Cut
BUG-040.after: A tool for trimming and cutting video files without re-encoding, preserving the original quality and codec data.

BUG-040.title: Hybrik API Reference
BUG-040.after: API reference documentation for Hybrik, a cloud-based media processing platform that provides endpoints for managing video encoding, transcoding, and delivery workflows.

BUG-040.title: DASH & HLS Sample Streams
BUG-040.after: Collection of sample streams in DASH and HLS formats provided by Bitmovin for testing adaptive bitrate streaming implementations and player functionality.

BUG-040.title: draft-pantos-hls-rfc8216bis-00 - HTTP Live Streaming 2nd Edition
BUG-040.after: Draft specification for the second edition of HTTP Live Streaming (HLS), documenting the protocol standard for adaptive bitrate video delivery over HTTP.

BUG-040.title: Live Video Transmuxing/Transcoding: FFmpeg vs TwitchTranscoder, Part I
BUG-040.after: Technical comparison examining FFmpeg and Twitch's proprietary transcoder for live video transmuxing and transcoding in production environments, Part 1.

BUG-040.title: Live Video Transmuxing/Transcoding: FFmpeg vs TwitchTranscoder, Part 2
BUG-040.after: Technical comparison examining FFmpeg and Twitch's proprietary transcoder for live video transmuxing and transcoding in production environments, Part 2.

BUG-040.title: Creating a Master Playlist | Apple Developer Documentation
BUG-040.after: Apple's developer documentation explaining how to construct HLS master playlists that reference multiple variant streams for adaptive bitrate video delivery.

BUG-040.after: Google developer tool for analyzing and measuring audio loudness levels in media content to meet broadcast and platform-specific audio standards.

BUG-040.title: Back to Basics: Encoding Definition and Adaptive Bitrate
BUG-040.after: Guide explaining encoding fundamentals and adaptive bitrate streaming concepts, including how to deliver multiple quality versions optimized for different network conditions.

BUG-040.title: HOW TO: View an HLS Stream in QuickTime or VLC – Softron Support Desk
BUG-040.after: Tutorial demonstrating how to playback HLS streams using QuickTime and VLC media players on desktop systems.

BUG-040.after: Bento4 developer documentation for HLS-specific tools, including utilities for creating, analyzing, and manipulating HTTP Live Streaming playlists and segments.

BUG-040.after: Bento4 is a comprehensive toolkit and C++ library providing command-line tools and APIs for MP4 and DASH media format creation, manipulation, and packaging.

BUG-040.after: Artillery.io plugin documentation for load testing and quality assurance of HTTP Live Streaming implementations and player performance.

BUG-040.after: Apple's official documentation explaining the architecture and components of HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) for video delivery.

BUG-040.after: Bitmovin's comprehensive guide covering digital rights management concepts, technologies, and implementation approaches for protecting video content.

BUG-040.after: ATSC technical standard documenting methods for measuring, establishing, and maintaining consistent audio loudness levels in digital television.

BUG-040.after: Tutorial on using FFmpeg's loudnorm filter based on EBU R128 standards to normalize and adjust audio levels in media files.

BUG-040.after: DASH Industry Forum's implementation guidelines document specifying interoperability points for DASH-based adaptive streaming systems.

BUG-040.after: Reference guide mapping x264 encoder options to FFmpeg parameters for video encoding on Linux systems.

BUG-040.after: Documentation for integrating Amazon S3 object storage with Fastly's CDN platform to optimize content delivery and distribution.

BUG-040.after: Technical specification for MPEG point cloud compression standards used in encoding and decoding three-dimensional point cloud data for efficient storage and transmission.
